landmark_structure.justification: Both skip links present at the top ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation'), appropriate for a sidebar layout where the nav is not at the top of the DOM. Two <nav> elements each carry distinct aria-label values ('Primary', 'Support'). <main> correctly wraps only the projects content and is outside the sidebar nav. No missing landmarks.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> 'Projects', followed by <h2> 'All projects' as a section heading, then <h3> for each project card name — a correct and logical hierarchy with no skipped levels. Adding the intermediate <h2> section label is a superior approach to jumping straight to card headings.

element_choice.justification: Button with popovertarget/popover API used for the user dropdown (not <details>/<summary>). <time> elements carry datetime attributes on all three dates. <article> used for self-contained project cards. <dl>/<dt>/<dd> used for card metadata key-value pairs. All interactive elements are native <button> or <a>.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where native HTML cannot provide the semantics: aria-label on both <nav> elements, aria-current='page' on the active nav link, aria-labelledby on the <section> and each <article>. tabindex='-1' on <main> is correct for skip-link targeting. No redundant role attributes anywhere; the visually-hidden badge text adds status label context without duplicating ARIA roles.

list_semantics.justification: Primary nav items are in a <ul>, secondary support nav items are in a <ul>, user dropdown items are in a <ul>, and the three project cards are wrapped in a <ul> — all collections where item count is meaningful to the user.

content_realism.justification: Realistic project names (Nebula Design System, Horizon Mobile App, Atlas Data Pipeline), varied member counts (8, 5, 12), and plausible recent dates. No placeholder text.

expectations_met: <main> wraps only the projects content area; sidebar nav is outside it
Skip links ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation') are the first focusable elements
Both <nav> elements have distinct aria-label values
User dropdown uses a <button> with the Popover API (popovertarget/popover attributes)
'Profile' and 'Preferences' are <a> links; 'Sign Out' is a <button>
<main> used for the projects content area
Project cards are in a <ul> list
Status badges use visible text labels not colour alone
Project card names use <h3>, which is appropriate given the intermediate <h2> section heading
Secondary nav section has aria-label='Support' distinguishing it from primary
Logo is a link to '/' with aria-label and an img (alt='' is acceptable since the link label covers it)
expectations_missed: Key expectation listed card names as <h2>, but the output uses <h3> under an <h2> 'All projects' section heading — the h3 is technically more correct given the hierarchy, so this is not a real miss
additional_observations: Nesting <nav aria-label='Support'> inside <nav aria-label='Primary'> is valid HTML but unconventional; a sibling structure or wrapping the sidebar in <aside> would be a common alternative
The visually-hidden '(active)' span inside each badge is mildly redundant since the visible badge text already conveys the status, but it is harmless
